title: senecio macedonicus subsp. macedonicus
intro: Senecio macedonicus subsp. macedonicus, also known as Macedonian ragwort, is a plant species within the daisy family that is native to the Balkan Peninsula. The plant has been found to possess medicinal properties due to its content of sesquiterpene lactones, which have been traditionally used to treat various health issues. The health benefits of Senecio macedonicus subsp. macedonicus include its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help to alleviate conditions such as fever, wounds, and respiratory issues. In traditional medicine, the plant is prepared as a decoction or infusion, which can be used as an herbal remedy for its medicinal properties.
